title: ProjectPicker stale-data masking during new scan
description:
ProjectPicker.svelte branch precedence:
  1. has discoveredProjects → show list
  2. else if scanning → 'Scanning for projects…'
  3. else if scan_roots configured → 'No projects found'
  4. else → no message

When discoveredProjects is non-empty from a PRIOR scan, branch 1 wins and the 'Scanning…' message at line ~36 never renders during a new scan. The user sees the list with no signal that a new scan is in flight.

The footer scan-status line ('Scanning <roots>…' / 'Scan directories: <roots>') at lines 58-59 IS visible alongside the list, but the smaller footer is the only scan-in-progress indicator on the happy-path-with-existing-data flow.

Fix options:
- Show 'Refreshing list…' overlay when scanning && discoveredProjects.length > 0
- Move the scanning indicator to a more prominent position
- Gray out the existing list during refresh

Detected by: PR #115 review (silent-failure-hunter M1). Rated minor.

See: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/components/ProjectPicker.svelte:23-44

title: FailedAgent variant naming asymmetry (kinds-of-agent vs kinds-of-failure)
description:
Variant naming on FailedAgent in crates/kiro-market-core/src/service/mod.rs:

  enum FailedAgent {
    Agent { ... },               // subject
    UnparseableAgent { ... },    // adjective + subject
    CompanionBundle { ... },     // different subject category
  }

The type name is FailedAgent. All three variants should read as kinds-of-failure, not kinds-of-agent. CompanionBundle reads as 'a companion bundle,' not 'a companion bundle failure.'

Parallel naming options:
  - AgentInstall / UnparseableAgentFile / CompanionBundleInstall (verb-suffix discipline)
  - Drop the suffix entirely: Agent / UnparseableFile / CompanionBundle (let kind tag carry the load)

This is a breaking change to the wire format: the kind discriminator values (agent / unparseable_agent / companion_bundle) would change. FE consumers and any external CLI consumers reading the JSON shape would need to migrate.

Detected by: PR #113 review (type-design-analyzer) + PR #115 review (mention). Rated mildest concern — the current naming is correct, just not perfectly parallel. Defer unless the wire format breaks for another reason.

See: crates/kiro-market-core/src/service/mod.rs:644-700 (enum definition)

title: Disk-vs-tracking cross-check for installed: bool in catalog reads
description:
The slice-1 design (docs/plans/2026-05-13-list-plugin-catalog-design.md) and today's list_skills_for_plugin both compute installed: bool as tracking-file membership only (browse.rs:896: installed.skills.contains_key(name)). The probe demonstrated the divergence: a user who hand-deletes .kiro/skills/<name>/ will see 'Installed' in the UI until tracking is rewritten.

Filed as the trigger-condition issue cited in non-goal #2 of the slice-1 design ('addressing it is its own slice'). Not blocking the redesign — only file the work if the misreport actually surfaces as a user-visible bug worth the perf cost.

Cost shape (rough):
- One std::fs::metadata() per item per category per catalog read.
- For a marketplace with 50 plugins each averaging 5 items per category: 750 stat() calls per catalog read. Negligible on local fs, possibly noticeable on slow network mounts.

Design choices (from cheap to thorough):
1. Add a optional 'verify_disk: bool' parameter to list_plugin_catalog; default false (today's behavior); UI opts in if/when the orphan UX bug is reported.
2. Always cross-check; accept the perf cost.
3. Add a separate 'verify_installed_state(project_path)' command that returns a divergence list, called on demand.

Probe artifact pinning today's behavior: crates/kiro-market-core/tests/prove_it_list_plugin_catalog.rs::probe_skills_orphan_disk_dir_diverges.

title: Retire BrowseTab Skills view; extend search to match item names
description:
Origin: early design pass for the BrowseTab redesign (commit 4601457 conversation, decision #3). When the Plugins-only grid + Customize drawer landed in slices 3-4 (commits ec24a8d, 16e5e48), we agreed the Skills view's primary use case (per-plugin curation) was fully covered by the drawer, and the toggle could be retired. The blocker was that the Skills view's secondary use case (cross-plugin item discovery via type-to-filter) needs an analogue in the Plugins view before the toggle can go away.

What the Skills view does today that the new BrowseTab doesn't:
- Type 'pdf' in the filter box → see every skill across all loaded plugins whose name or description contains 'pdf'. The Plugins-view search currently only matches plugin name + description, so a skill called 'pdf-processor' inside a plugin called 'documents' wouldn't surface.
- Multi-select skills across plugins, then click 'Install N selected' → batch-install picks across plugins. Niche power-user workflow.

Scope of this issue:
1. Extend the BrowseTab.svelte search predicate to also match against entry.skills[].name/description, entry.steering[].name, entry.agents[].name/description. A plugin should appear in the filtered grid if EITHER the plugin metadata OR any of its items matches the query.
2. Surface why a plugin matched when it was via an item name (e.g., a small 'matched: pdf-processor' sub-line on the card) so users understand the appearance and can find the skill via Customize.
3. Remove the Plugins/Skills view toggle (browseView state) from BrowseTab.svelte.
4. Remove the per-skill multi-select selection set (selectedSkills SvelteSet, toggleSkill, installSelected, the 'Install N selected' footer button).
5. Remove SkillCard.svelte and its imports.
6. Update e2e (tests/e2e/app.spec.ts uses 'filter skills by name or description...' placeholder text — would change to 'Search plugins by name or item...').

Loss accepted: cross-plugin batch install via Skills-view multi-select. Power-user workflow; if anyone reports missing it, file a separate issue for a bulk-install UI on the Plugins view.

Estimated work: ~half-day for the search extension + match-reason rendering, ~half-day for the deletion sweep + e2e fixup.

Status: open, not blocking other work. Surfaces during normal BrowseTab use only as 'I miss being able to search inside skills'.

title: F4: Improve orphan-file remediation message UX
description:
The current AgentError::OrphanFileAtDestination Display (crates/kiro-market-core/src/error.rs:376-379):

  file exists at `{path}` but has no tracking entry; remove it manually or pass --force

is poor advice when the orphan is a git-tracked file in the user's repo (the kiro-control-center case that triggered PR #113). A better message would:

- Acknowledge that orphans may be intentional repo content (not stray junk)
- Suggest force-install OR moving the install to a different target
- Avoid implying the user should delete a tracked file

UX-text change in the AgentError Display impl. No type changes; no wire-format migration.

Higher-leverage alternative: F5 (detect git-tracked at install time and produce a specific message). F4 is the floor; F5 is the ceiling.

Why deferred from PR #113: pure UX text, doesn't depend on or affect the wire-format change.

See: docs/plans/2026-05-09-failed-agent-discriminator-design.md F4, crates/kiro-market-core/src/error.rs:373-380

title: F1: Engine-level multi-conflict collection in classify_companion_collision
description:
Today classify_companion_collision (crates/kiro-market-core/src/project.rs:3106) returns the first conflict and bails. The wire-format field FailedAgent::CompanionBundle.conflicts is shape-compatible with multi-conflict (Vec<PathBuf>) but in practice ships length-0 or length-1 only.

Implementation: walk every rel_path before returning, accumulate every PathOwnedByOtherPlugin / OrphanFileAtDestination collision into a Vec, then return the accumulated set as a single AgentError variant or as a new collected-conflicts variant. companion_conflicts_from_error in service/mod.rs:739 already handles the projection; the engine change is independent.

Forward-compat: no wire-format migration required — the Vec already exists.

Why deferred from PR #113: independent engine work; the wire-format change shipped without it because the FE consumes conflicts as opaque list (length-1 today, length-N tomorrow).

See: docs/plans/2026-05-09-failed-agent-discriminator-design.md F1, crates/kiro-market-core/src/project.rs:3106-3171

title: FailedAgent wire-format follow-ups (F1-F5 from PR #113 design)
description:
Five follow-on items carved out at design time during PR #113 (refactor(service): make FailedAgent a tagged enum). All five are documented in docs/plans/2026-05-09-failed-agent-discriminator-design.md sections F1-F5 with rationale for why each was deferred from the wire-format change.

Scope of this epic: track the F1-F5 items as discrete child issues so each can be picked up independently when its dependencies / scheduling align. No work in this epic blocks any other.

See: docs/plans/2026-05-09-failed-agent-discriminator-design.md:153 (F1 onwards)

title: code-reviewer.md cross-prompt references — add file paths to prevent rename rot
description:
code-reviewer.md (around line 39) tells the standalone code-reviewer to consult silent-failure-hunter.md, type-design-analyzer.md, pr-test-analyzer.md, comment-analyzer.md as references. The current text mentions these prompts by bare name without file paths.

Failure mode: if a specialist prompt is renamed or its file moves, this cross-reference silently rots. Reader following the guidance can't find the cited prompts via grep.

Fix options:
- Link with file paths: '.kiro/agents/prompts/silent-failure-hunter.md' etc.
- Or omit the 'use their prompts as reference' instruction entirely and just say 'cover those domains yourself'

Detected by: PR #115 review (comment-analyzer #5). Rated minor.

See: .kiro/agents/prompts/code-reviewer.md:~39

title: Defense-in-depth: remove commands should verify plugin ownership before unlinking
description:
Companion to the catalog ownership fix (commit 94bddc7) for the cross-plugin same-name bug from the terax-ai project.

The catalog fix scopes the per-item  flag by plugin+marketplace ownership, which closes the destructive UI flow (the drawer no longer shows another plugin's tracking entry as checked, so the user can't accidentally uncheck+Apply to remove it).

But the underlying remove commands still trust caller-supplied names without verifying ownership:
- commands.removeSkill(name, project_path)
- commands.removeSteeringFile(name, project_path)
- commands.removeAgent(name, project_path)

A future caller (CLI, script, programmatic consumer, future UI feature) could still accidentally remove the wrong plugin's items by passing the wrong name. The catalog UI fix covers the drawer's path; this is the belt-and-braces backend guard.

Proposed shape (matches commands::installed::remove_skill's existing pattern, extended with optional ownership context):

  removeSkill(name, project_path, expected_plugin: Option<String>, expected_marketplace: Option<String>) -> Result<(), CommandError>

When the optional context is provided, KiroProject::remove_skill checks that the tracking entry's plugin/marketplace matches before unlinking; on mismatch returns SkillError::OwnerMismatch (new variant) rather than silently removing.

When the optional context is absent (CLI / legacy callers), the behavior matches today's name-only remove (preserves the API surface).

The drawer's apply-diff path would pass the (marketplace, plugin) context it already has, getting the defense automatically.

Same shape for removeSteeringFile and removeAgent.

Estimated work: ~2-3 hours. Three Tauri command signature widenings + three project-side ownership checks + parallel error variants per category + per-category fence tests.

Status: not blocking — the catalog fix already prevents the user-visible destructive flow. File this for the next backlog grooming pass; promote if anyone reports a similar cross-plugin destruction via a non-drawer code path.

title: Remove 'What changed from v1' sections from review-toolkit prompts (post-v2 settling)
description:
Seven prompts opened with a 'What changed from v1' rationale block during the PR #114 (v2 dual-mode protocol) refactor:

  code-reviewer.md:7-15
  code-simplifier.md:7-9
  comment-analyzer.md:7-13
  pr-test-analyzer.md:7-13
  silent-failure-hunter.md:7-13
  type-design-analyzer.md:7-13
  review-orchestrator.md:7-11

Total ~35 lines across the 7 prompts. These sections were load-bearing for reviewing PR #114 itself, but they consume agent context on EVERY invocation. code-simplifier.md's block even admits 'No structural changes from v1 here; this header just tracks the v2 lineage.'

Fix: delete the 'What changed from v1' sections once v2 has settled (say, 30 days post-merge or after a few real review cycles confirm the dual-mode protocol is stable). Git history remains the durable record of what changed.

Detected by: PR #115 review (code-simplifier #2). Rated low.

See: 7 specialist + orchestrator prompts under .kiro/agents/prompts/

title: F5: Detect git-tracked destination files at install time
description:
Higher-leverage version of F4. At install time, when an OrphanFileAtDestination check fires, also run `git ls-files --error-unmatch <path>` to detect whether the orphan is git-tracked. If git claims the file, the remediation message can be specific:

  this file is git-tracked in your repo; force-install will produce a diff in your working tree

vs the generic F4 message.

ARCHITECTURAL CONSTRAINT: introduces a `git` subprocess dependency at the install layer. crates/kiro-market-core/src/CLAUDE.md says 'domain core stays free of external runtimes' — git subprocess does NOT belong in kiro-market-core. The check therefore has to live in:

- The Tauri command layer (crates/kiro-control-center/src-tauri/src/commands/), OR
- The CLI install flow (crates/kiro-market/src/commands/install.rs)

Both options require flowing 'is git-tracked?' from outside the core into the install path's collision-check fork point. That's a non-trivial API change to the install entry points.

If F4 lands first as a no-op-fallback UX improvement, F5 layers on top as the 'when git is present, use this better message' enhancement.

Why deferred from PR #113: violates 'domain core stays free' rule; designable but not designed.

See: docs/plans/2026-05-09-failed-agent-discriminator-design.md F5

title: Remove plugin-actions.ts diagnostic when F3 lands (same commit)
description:
When F3 (kiro-kmj4) ships the inline per-failure UI in InstalledTab.svelte, the temporary diagnostic console.error block in plugin-actions.ts (around line 130-149) MUST be removed in the same commit as the inline UI lands — not as a separate cleanup PR.

Why same-commit coupling matters:

If the diagnostic is removed first (cleanup PR before F3 ships):
  - Per-item failure visibility vanishes from the app entirely
  - Users see only the count summary ('1 steering failed · 8 agents failed') with no way to inspect WHY each item failed

If the diagnostic is removed after F3 ships (cleanup PR after):
  - Users briefly see both DevTools console.error AND inline rendering
  - Minor noise but no information loss

The diagnostic's job IS to be temporary scaffolding. Coupling its removal to F3's arrival is the cleanest single-commit boundary.

This issue is BLOCKED by kiro-kmj4 (F3). When F3 is opened for implementation, link this issue as the cleanup contract — the F3 PR should include a checkbox 'Remove plugin-actions.ts diagnostic per kiro-5qcb'.

See: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/plugin-actions.ts lines 130-149 (Temporary diagnostic block)
notes: Closed: PR #118: console.error diagnostic removed alongside the inline UI (same-commit coupling honored)

title: F3: Inline per-failure UI in InstalledTab.svelte consuming FailedAgent wire shape
notes: Closed: PR #118: inline per-failure UI in InstalledTab.svelte + BrowseTab.svelte

title: F2: FailedSteeringFile parallel tagged-enum restructure
description:
Apply the same 3-variant tagged-enum pattern that FailedAgent now uses to FailedSteeringFile, IF AND WHEN steering grows a bundle-level concept (e.g. multi-file atomic steering installs). Today steering is single-file per install, so the parallel doesn't apply yet.

Trigger: a steering-bundle feature request lands. At that point the FailedSteeringFile flat shape will start carrying nullable / dual-purpose fields, which is the exact smell PR #113 fixed for FailedAgent.

Why deferred from PR #113: YAGNI — no bundle concept in steering today. Restructuring before there's a use case repeats the over-engineering anti-pattern.

See: docs/plans/2026-05-09-failed-agent-discriminator-design.md F2, crates/kiro-market-core/src/service/mod.rs FailedSteeringFile (current flat shape)

title: ScanRootsPanel z-index conflict hides global toast on scan failure
description:
When the ScanRootsPanel modal is open and a scan failure fires, the projectError feedback only surfaces via the global toast at +page.svelte (lines ~133-143). The toast has z-50; ScanRootsPanel also has z-50. Whichever renders later wins the stacking order.

Failure mode: user opens ScanRootsPanel, triggers a scan that fails, toast renders BEHIND the panel and is invisible. User sees no error feedback.

Fix: bump the toast to z-[60] (or pick any value > 50). One-line CSS change.

Detected by: PR #115 review (silent-failure-hunter M2). Rated minor — only manifests when the panel is open AND a scan fails AND the toast was supposed to surface the error.

See: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/components/ScanRootsPanel.svelte (z-50 on the modal), crates/kiro-control-center/src/routes/+page.svelte:133-143 (global toast, also z-50)

title: plugin-actions.ts: add line range to InstalledTab cite
description:
plugin-actions.ts (around line 138-139) cites InstalledTab.svelte as the target shape for inline failure rendering:

  see runPluginRemove's per-failure <details> panel in InstalledTab.svelte for the target shape

No line number. Structural claim that will rot if InstalledTab is reorganized before F3 (kiro-kmj4) ships.

Fix: add a line range or section anchor — e.g. 'InstalledTab.svelte:NNN — the per-failure <details> block'.

Bonus: when F3 lands and this entire comment block is deleted alongside the diagnostic (per kiro-5qcb coupling), this concern disappears.

Detected by: PR #115 review (comment-analyzer #6). Rated minor rot risk.

See: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/plugin-actions.ts:138-139
notes: Closed: PR #118: moot — the entire comment block kiro-jkk6 flagged was removed across commits cd80244 + c7b0330

title: code-reviewer.md: unify mode-marker phrasings (3 variants in one file)
description:
.kiro/agents/prompts/code-reviewer.md uses three different phrasings for the same orchestrator-invoked mode detection within one file (line numbers approximate post-merge):

  L14: 'Present → orchestrator-driven mode'
  L45: 'Look at your relevant_context for the literal marker string [orchestrator-invoked]'
  L54: 'does not contain the [orchestrator-invoked] marker'

All three are correct and refer to the same protocol, but the wording drift inside one file invites copy-paste errors when the protocol is extended.

Fix: pick one canonical phrasing ('marker present/absent in relevant_context') and use it verbatim in all three places.

Detected by: PR #115 review (comment-analyzer #4). Rated minor.

See: .kiro/agents/prompts/code-reviewer.md

title: DRY: hoist Specialist Output discipline block from specialist prompts to steering doc
description:
Four specialist prompts carry a near-identical 9-line 'Output discipline' block:

  pr-test-analyzer.md:15-23
  type-design-analyzer.md:15-23
  comment-analyzer.md:15-23
  silent-failure-hunter.md:15-23

The block enumerates what specialists must NOT emit (Independent Assessment, Holistic PR Assessment, Verdict — all orchestrator-only). Only the domain noun changes ('test changes' / 'type changes' / etc.).

One drift case already exists: silent-failure-hunter's trailing 'No Holistic Assessment...' line is welded onto a different paragraph than the other three.

Fix: hoist the universal 3 bullets into a 'Specialist Output Discipline' section in .kiro/steering/review-process.md (auto-loaded into every agent's context per its opening sentence). Each specialist keeps only a one-line domain-specific reminder + reference.

Detected by: PR #115 review (code-simplifier #1). Rated low — DRY win, no behavior change.

See: 4 specialist prompts under .kiro/agents/prompts/, .kiro/steering/review-process.md

title: Surface plugin name in InstalledTab failure-panel headings (both panels)
description:
Both the install-failure panel and the remove-failure panel in InstalledTab.svelte have headings that don't name the plugin. Currently:

- Install panel (lines ~354-394): "Show failures"
- Remove panel (lines ~280-324): "Show items + failures" / "Show items"

The banner above provides plugin context, but in a long plugin list the banner may scroll out of view from the panel. Both panel headings should incorporate the plugin name from their already-carried state vars (`installResultPlugin` / `removeResultPlugin`).

Both must be updated in the same commit to preserve the twin-pattern discipline established in F3 — adding plugin-name to only one panel would create the install/remove asymmetry that F3's fixup commit 01ef321 just restored.

Suggested heading text:
- Install panel: "Show failures for <plugin>"
- Remove panel: "Show items + failures for <plugin>" / "Show items for <plugin>"

Detected by: F3 code-quality review (PR opening kiro-kmj4, commit c7b0330). Reviewer rated the original finding as Important but accepted the deferral because the right fix touches both panels at once.

See: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/components/InstalledTab.svelte:280-324 (remove panel), :354-394 (install panel after F3)
design: Single commit touching both `<details>` summary text lines in InstalledTab.svelte. Both variables already exist; no state plumbing. The remove panel's summary already has conditional text ("Show items + failures" vs "Show items") — extend each branch to append ` for ${removeResultPlugin}`. Install panel currently has just "Show failures" — append ` for ${installResultPlugin}`. Manual smoke verifies both panels show plugin name correctly after Update and Remove.

title: PR #115 review polish backlog
description:
Nine 'nice to have' items surfaced during the multi-agent review of PR #115 (test/failed-agent-coverage-gaps). None is blocking; pick up opportunistically. All rated Minor/Suggestion by the reviewers.

These split into three rough categories:

1. UI race / display polish (FE):
   - Concurrent refreshProjects() race (scanGeneration counter)
   - ProjectPicker stale-data masking during new scan
   - ScanRootsPanel z-index conflict with global toast

2. Review-toolkit prompt / steering hygiene:
   - code-reviewer.md inconsistent mode-marker phrasings
   - code-reviewer.md cross-prompt references without file paths
   - plugin-actions.ts:138 InstalledTab cite without line number
   - DRY: hoist Specialist Output discipline block to steering doc
   - 'What changed from v1' sections cleanup (post-v2 settling)

3. Type-design polish:
   - FailedAgent variant naming asymmetry (Agent / UnparseableAgent / CompanionBundle reads as kinds-of-agent, not kinds-of-failure)

See: PR #115 multi-agent review aggregated report (referenced in /pr-review-toolkit:review-pr output from 2026-05-10)

title: Concurrent refreshProjects() race — add scanGeneration counter
description:
refreshProjects() in crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/stores/project.svelte.ts sets store.scanning = true at entry and false in finally. Two concurrent calls (e.g. rapid scan-root add/remove) share the single boolean — the first's finally fires while the second is still mid-scan, prematurely flipping scanning back to false.

Failure mode: cosmetic only. Spinner disappears briefly while a scan is still in flight. No data corruption (the second call's result.discoveredProjects write happens last regardless of which scan finished first, which IS a separate issue but small).

Fix sketch: introduce a scanGeneration: number on store, increment at refreshProjects entry, snapshot the value, only flip scanning=false in finally if gen still matches snapshot. Also only write discoveredProjects on the most-recent generation's result.

Detected by: PR #115 review (silent-failure-hunter I1, code-reviewer concern). Rated cosmetic.

See: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/stores/project.svelte.ts:85-110 (post-PR-114 catch block)

title: Per-item steering/agent install/remove Tauri commands for Customize drawer
description:
The BrowseTab redesign's Customize drawer (slice 4, shipped 2026-05-14) renders steering and agents sections as read-only because today the bindings only support whole-plugin install (installPlugin), whole-plugin steering install (installPluginSteering), per-skill install (installSkills), and per-skill remove (removeSkill). NO per-file steering install/remove and NO per-agent install/remove exist.

User-visible symptom (the trigger to prioritize this): a plugin that ships only steering and/or agents (no skills) opens in the drawer with every checkbox disabled. Slice 4 added an explanatory banner pointing users at the whole-plugin Install button as the workaround, but Customize on a steering-only plugin remains a dead-end UX-wise.

Required Tauri commands (signatures TBD during their own design pass):
- install_steering_files(marketplace, plugin, names: Vec<String>, force, project_path) -> InstallSteeringResult
- remove_steering_file(plugin, name, project_path) -> Result<(), CommandError>
- install_agent(marketplace, plugin, name, force, project_path) -> InstallAgentResult
- remove_agent(plugin, name, project_path) -> Result<(), CommandError>

Each requires a corresponding kiro-market-core service method, validated newtypes for fields crossing FFI, and the typical _impl(svc, ...) testable shape per CLAUDE.md.

Implementation shape mirrors slice 1 (which shipped list_plugin_catalog_for_marketplace via three new service methods + one Tauri command + a binding-shape fence). Estimated ~1 day of focused work for the four commands; the drawer wiring changes are an additional ~half-day to widen the diff shape and apply handler.

Once landed, the drawer's Steering and Agents sections become interactive (parallel to today's Skills section), the banner from slice 4 is removed, and skills-less plugins regain a meaningful Customize/Manage drawer.

Status: unblocked since slice 1 shipped (4601457 → 010c489 commit chain on main); the gating prereq is gone. Ready to start.
